name: Sequence-to-Sequence (Seq2Seq)
training_approach: supervised training on parallel corpora (e.g., translation pairs)
lineage_path: McCulloch-Pitts neuron -> Perceptron -> MLP -> RNN -> LSTM -> Seq2Seq
summary: Sequence-to-Sequence learning introduced the encoder-decoder framework where one LSTM encodes an input sequence into a fixed-length vector and another LSTM decodes it into an output sequence of arbitrary length. Proposed by Sutskever, Vinyals, and Le at Google in 2014, it enabled end-to-end neural machine translation and established the encoder-decoder pattern that directly evolved into the transformer architecture. The addition of attention mechanisms to Seq2Seq by Bahdanau et al. was the immediate precursor to 'Attention Is All You Need.'
